name: Jar Yue Har Guen (Deep-fried Fish and Prawn)
recipeInstructions:
1. Shell and de-vein the prawns.
2. Skin the fish fillets using a sharp knife. Depending on the size of the fillets, cut them into two, three, or four strips.
3. Wrap each strip around a prawn and secure with a wooden toothpick.
4. Mix egg with ginger and salt in a bowl.
5. Dip the fish rolls into plain flour, then beaten egg mixture, and finally breadcrumbs.
6. Heat about half a cup of oil in a wok until it forms a haze.
7. Add the fish rolls to the hot oil, frying them in batches of about 6 for approximately 3 minutes, or until golden brown all over.
8. Remove the fried rolls with a slotted spoon and drain on absorbent paper.
9. Repeat steps 7-8 for remaining rolls.
10. Serve hot with soy or chili sauce for dipping. Note: Choose flat fillets of firm white fish that are thin to ensure easy rolling around the prawns.
